methodsAttempted.query: site:lehigh.edu Lehigh University artificial intelligence policy students academic integrity generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official Lehigh AI, LTS, research, and news pages. The AI policy/best-practices page, guiding-principles page, LTS faculty guidance, and research guidance were selected for verification.
The official Artificial Intelligence at Lehigh subdomain provided central AI policies/best practices and guiding principles.
The Library & Technology Services subdomain provided official faculty classroom guidance on generative AI.
The Office of the Vice Provost for Research subdomain provided official guidance on generative AI in human-subjects research.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found Lehigh-controlled AI policy/best-practices, guiding-principles, faculty classroom guidance, and human-subjects research guidance pages. A generic AI hub and a news article were documented but rejected for low policy specificity or news/advisory scope.

targets.sourceTitle: CITL Guidance for Faculty on Generative AI in the Classroom
Guidance: Generative AI and Human Subjects Research

stopConditions: Stop after official Lehigh pages support direct claim candidates.
Do not infer binding policy from the guiding-principles page because it states the principles are not policy statements or a mandate.
Do not use news/event pages as policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Lehigh tells faculty, staff, students, and affiliates that institutional, restricted, or critical data may not be submitted to online systems including generative AI tools.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Do not submit institutional, restricted, or critical data to generative AI or other online information systems.

artifacts.sourceTitle: CITL Guidance for Faculty on Generative AI in the Classroom | Library & Technology Services
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Directly address generative AI in your classroom, in your syllabi, in each assignment prompt, and in course materials. Provide students with explicit guidance about whether and how they may use AI-powered tools in your class.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Lehigh CITL guidance tells faculty to directly address AI and explain whether and how students may use AI-powered tools.

artifacts.claimText: Lehigh's human-subjects research guidance says use of AI to analyze or process data must be disclosed to participants during consent.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI analysis or processing of human-subjects research data must be disclosed in the participant consent process.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance: Generative AI and Human Subjects Research | Office of the Vice Provost for Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Use of AI to analyze or process data must be disclosed to participants as part of the consent process. The consent language must describe which AI tools will be used during or after the study, and whether AI tools will be used to process identifiable or de-identified data.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Lehigh research guidance requires AI data analysis or processing to be disclosed during consent.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Lehigh says AI services acceptable for Class II data may be used in human-subjects research upon IRB approval, while unapproved services should be reviewed with LTS for data-security requirements.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Class II acceptable AI services require IRB approval for human-subjects research use; unapproved services should be checked with LTS.

artifacts.title: Lehigh University AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Official Lehigh sources support staged claims about restricted data in generative AI systems, breach reporting, course-specific student AI rules, faculty guidance for classroom AI use, and human-subjects research disclosure/IRB conditions. The AI guiding-principles page was treated as contextual guidance because it explicitly says the principles are not policy statements or a mandate.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The guiding-principles page was not treated as binding policy because the page says the principles are not policy statements or a mandate.
The Lehigh news article discovered by search was rejected as news/advisory material and was not used as policy evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
